game_description: Avoid drawing the Exploding Kitten card — last player alive wins.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Count your Skips, Attacks, and Nopes immediately. A hand with 2+ Skips is strong — you can avoid drawing for multiple turns. A Defuse without any skip/attack cards means you must draw often and can defuse only once. Plan your aggression level accordingly.

Play Skip when the deck is large and unshuffled — drawing is low risk anyway. Save Skips for when the deck is thin or you've seen (via Peek) that an Exploding Kitten is near the top. Early Skips are mostly wasted; mid-to-late Skips save lives.
strategy_stealth: Early Skips = low value, save them
Play Skip when deck is thin or peeked

strategies.strategy_detailed: Nope's highest value: cancel an Attack onto you (prevents 2-turn stack) or a Shuffle that erases your Peek info. In multi-player, Noping a Skip just delays the player one turn — usually wasted. In heads-up, Noping their Skip forces a draw and can kill them outright.

strategies.condition: Deck position is known or dangerous
strategies.strategy_detailed: Shuffle resets knowledge of deck order — use it when you know an Exploding Kitten is near the top and you lack a Defuse. In multi-player, shuffling benefits everyone; do it only when you need it, not to 'help' the table.
strategy_stealth: Shuffle when kitten is near top
Only shuffle when you need it

strategies.strategy_detailed: Attack the player who is strongest or most likely to survive long. In multi-player, attacking the weakest target is often a waste — they'll die soon anyway. Attack forces the target to draw twice; aim this at players with known-empty defenses.
strategy_stealth: Weak players die anyway — skip them

strategies.condition: You have only one Defuse left
strategies.strategy_detailed: With one Defuse, protect it at all costs. Play Skips and Attacks aggressively to avoid drawing. When you do draw and survive without using the Defuse, that's ideal. If forced to draw, bury the Exploding Kitten near the bottom to buy time for others to shuffle.
strategy_stealth: One Defuse: avoid drawing at all costs
Bury kitten near bottom if used

strategies.condition: Using a See the Future card
strategies.strategy_detailed: Peek gives you the top 3 cards' order. Note if an Exploding Kitten appears and its position. Keep this information private — don't react visibly. Use it to decide: Skip if kitten is top 3, draw normally if safe. Share with allies only if it benefits you.
strategy_stealth: Share peek only if it benefits you

strategies.strategy_detailed: In heads-up, Attacks are doubled draws. Play Attack only if you're confident the opponent lacks a Defuse. Nope your opponent's Skips to force them to draw. The player with more Defuse cards almost always wins — count what cards were played to estimate their hand.
strategy_stealth: Nope opponent Skips to force draws
Count Defuses played to estimate theirs

strategies.strategy_detailed: Heads-up placement depends on opponent's cards. Top: forces their next draw (if they lack Skip/Attack). Position 2: catches them only if they Skip (else YOU draw it). Position 3: catches them if they Attack you. Bottom is safe but lets them stack cards across many turns.
strategy_stealth: Pos 2: catches Skip; pos 3: catches Attack
Bottom safe but lets them stack

strategies.strategy_detailed: When you have 1-2 action cards left, evaluate each play carefully. A Nope used to cancel a trivial card leaves you defenseless against a future Attack. Conserve your last Skip for when you've peeked and confirmed the kitten is top 3.
strategy_stealth: Last Skip: save for confirmed danger
